title: Memory Allocation for Multiple Stacks in a Single Array
description: A visual representation of how two stacks can share a single array by growing from opposite ends towards the middle, efficiently utilizing memory.
relationships: Stack 1 grows from the beginning (left) of the array towards the end (right)
Stack 2 grows from the end (right) of the array towards the beginning (left)
A stack overflow occurs for either stack only when their top pointers meet and the free memory segment is exhausted
